_provenance.note: Refreshed from src/data/issues/1944.json after Phase 6 re-audit. Three corrections vs v1: education-budget ratio 66% → 37% (MOE Budget 2026 RM66.2B verified); dropped 'Article 117 abolished' (unrelated to political financing; repealed 1976 re constituency delimitation); removed chronologically impossible Jan 6 2025 post-verdict rally. v1 preserved at engine/output/najib-1mdb-tanore-stage1.v1-pre-phase6-corrections.json.
_provenance.audit_target: Audit the cards below — these are the final publishable content, not a draft.

headline: RM2.27 Billion, 25 Charges, One Verdict — and the Bill Nobody Mentions
context: Najib Razak convicted on all 25 charges over RM2.27 billion from 1MDB on Dec 26, 2025. 15 years jail, RM11.38 billion fine. RM24.46 billion in 1MDB debt still owed through 2039.

cards.big: One side has 76 witnesses and forged Saudi letters. The other has rally T-shirts.
cards.sub: Both miss the RM42 billion debt and the patronage system every party still uses.

cards.big: Judge declared four Saudi donation letters forgeries. Najib convicted on all 25 charges.
cards.sub: Statutory fine: 5× the gratification. Total: RM11.38 billion. Appeal filed December 30.
cards._supporting_detail: Kuala Lumpur High Court, Justice Collin Lawrence Sequerah, Dec 26, 2025. Verdict covered 4 abuse-of-power charges (Section 23(1) MACC Act 2009) + 21 money laundering charges (AMLATFPUAA 2001). Judge called Saudi donation defence 'incapable of belief'; evidence ruled 'cold, hard and incontrovertible.' Sentences: 15 years per abuse-of-power charge, 5 years per money laundering charge, concurrent. Fine: RM11.38B (5× the RM2.27B gratification). Additional RM2.08B ordered recovered under AMLATFPUAA. Defence counsel Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah filed notice of appeal Dec 30, 2025.

cards.big: RM42 billion in 1MDB debt paid. B40 single mothers bear the sharpest cost.
cards.sub: RM24.46 billion still owed through 2039 — more than a third of the 2026 education budget.
cards._supporting_detail: MOF parliamentary reply Nov 27, 2025: RM24.46B in outstanding 1MDB liabilities as of end-Sept 2025 (RM9.02B IMTN sukuk + RM15.44B shareholder advances). 30-year bonds mature in 2039. Government has paid RM42.17B to service 1MDB debt to date. Malaysia's 2026 Budget allocates RM66.2B to the Ministry of Education (largest single allocation; source: The Star, FMT, Scoop, Oct 10 2025). RM24.46B / RM66.2B = 37%. B40 women-led household cost: OECD and World Bank research on fiscal consolidation burden distribution. PM Anwar stated Dec 2 2025: '1MDB is still the government's biggest debt' (FMT).

cards.big: PH, BN, PN — every party runs on patronage. None will dismantle it voluntarily.
cards.sub: A political financing bill already exists. No party in power has moved to pass it.
cards._supporting_detail: All-Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) on Reform drafted a political financing bill; not tabled by any ruling coalition. C4 Centre (Cynthia Gabriel) called the conviction 'a critical step toward accountability' but urged GLC governance reform, political financing regulation, and Jho Low extradition from China (FMT, Borneo Post). PAC 2016 1MDB findings recommended structural reforms largely unimplemented. UMNO (BN), PKR/DAP/Amanah (PH), Bersatu/PAS (PN) have all historically run on patronage networks (academic sources + Global Witness).

cards.big: Najib is convicted. Patronage itself is untouched.
cards._supporting_detail: Reframes from individual-accountability narrative to system-accountability narrative. Najib's conviction is a legitimate High Court finding on the public record. The editorial reframe asks: what structural change has the conviction catalysed? Answer per C4 Centre + PAC: none so far.

cards.big: Connected elites of all races benefit. B40 households — disproportionately women-led — pay.
cards._supporting_detail: Synthesis of: (a) cross-community elite patronage as documented by Global Witness + C4 Centre reports; (b) 1MDB debt-servicing cost distribution falling on B40 through constrained fiscal space; (c) OECD/World Bank research on gendered incidence of fiscal consolidation.

sources: Al Jazeera (Dec 26 2025), CNN (Dec 26 2025), Malay Mail (Dec 26 2025 + Dec 30 2025), The Star (Dec 26 2025 + Oct 10 2025), The Edge Malaysia (Dec 26 2025 + MOF parliamentary reply Nov 27 2025), Free Malaysia Today (Dec 26 2025 + Dec 2 2025), PBS NewsHour (Dec 26 2025), Bloomberg (Dec 26 2025), SCMP (Dec 26 2025, Jan 2026), Nikkei Asia (Jan 2026), Malaysiakini (Dec 26 2025 + Jan 6 2025), BBC News (Dec 26 2025), Bernama (Nov 28 2025), Britannica, The Vibes, The Diplomat, Manila Times, MOF Malaysia, US Department of Justice (1MDB civil forfeiture), Global Witness, C4 Centre, OECD fiscal consolidation research, World Bank gendered cost of fiscal policy, APPG political financing bill draft, Auditor-General Malaysia 1MDB declassified report, PAC 2016 1MDB report
confidence: High — Verdict and sentence verified against 10+ independent outlets. RM24.46B outstanding debt figure traces to MOF parliamentary written reply Nov 27 2025. RM66.2B MOE 2026 allocation verified against Budget 2026 (The Star + FMT + Scoop, Oct 10 2025). No hedging on established facts; structural claims about patronage are framed as editorial synthesis, not fact.
